Title: Localising enzymes to biomolecular condensates increases their accumulation and benefits engineered metabolic pathway performance in Nicotiana benthamiana

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in agroinfiltrated mature leaves; condensates visible from 2 dpi and strongest around 3–5 dpi
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Intrinsically disordered RGG domain from LAF-1; phase-separation scaffold fused to mClover3 and SYNZIP1, with optional NLS
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus or cytosol depending on NLS presence
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Citramalate: NA; PHB: biodegradable carbon-storage polymer with industrial bioplastic value
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Engineered biomolecular condensates in leaf cell cytosol or nucleus; PHB shown to co-localize with condensates
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RGG scaffold enabled synthetic biomolecular condensate formation that increased heterologous enzyme accumulation and improved citramalate and PHB production relative to non-condensate controls
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression; confocal microscopy; -RGG deletion controls; FRAP; concentration-dependence analysis; metabolite/polymer quantification; Nile red co-localization; immunoblotting
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SYNZIP2-fused clients including mCherry; MdCMS; PhbA; PhbB; PhbC
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium infiltration; P19 co-infiltration; re-infiltration for PHB experiments; observations at 2–13 dpi
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The LAF-1-derived RGG scaffold formed synthetic nuclear and cytosolic condensates in N. benthamiana, whereas -RGG controls did not. 2. The condensates behaved as liquid-like multicomponent phase-separating systems in vivo. 3. Recruitment of metabolic enzymes to these condensates increased product accumulation for both citramalate and PHB pathways. 4. Condensate localization increased client enzyme accumulation, likely through enhanced stability/protection from proteolysis.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Protein sequestration may not suit all recombinant proteins, for example targets requiring ER N-glycosylation; no direct negative growth effect reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A general synthetic compartmentation platform for improving enzyme stability, product sequestration and metabolic productivity in N. benthamiana biofactory applications
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a synthetic scaffold rather than a native plant metabolic gene, but it is a core experimental determinant of nutrient/metabolite output in the study

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-infiltrated transiently in mature leaves during expression experiments
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf tissue under transient expression conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Used to increase transgene expression levels supporting accumulation of heterologous enzymes and metabolite production, but not itself a direct metabolic determinant tested in Results
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. P19 was used as an auxiliary expression booster during agroinfiltration. 2. The paper did not test P19 as a focal determinant of metabolite accumulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included only as a methodological accessory; not a true core gene from the Results section

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in agroinfiltrated mature leaves; assessed at 5 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Citramalate synthase; α-isopropylmalate synthase-like family; N-terminal chloroplast transit peptide removed for cytosolic targeting; fused to SYNZIP2 and mCherry
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Single-step heterologous citramalate production from central carbon metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses acetyl-CoA and pyruvate to form citramalate and CoA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cytosolic biomolecular condensates in agroinfiltrated leaf cells were engineered; direct citramalate sequestration not demonstrated
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Yes in the engineered one-step pathway; sole catalytic step producing citramalate
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Engineered to cytosolic biomolecular condensates; native enzyme is plastid-localized in apple
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium infiltration in mature leaves; co-infiltration with P19 silencing suppressor; 5 dpi sampling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Active in N. benthamiana and condensate targeting increased citramalate accumulation about 2.3-fold relative to diffuse cytosolic localization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression; condensate co-localization imaging; metabolite profiling by GC-MS; comparison of +RGG versus -RGG scaffold; semi-quantitative fluorescence accumulation analysis
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SYNZIP1 scaffold; mCherry; RGG-based condensate scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Truncated MdCMS lacking the plastid transit peptide was successfully recruited to cytosolic synthetic condensates in N. benthamiana. 2. MdCMS remained catalytically active when condensate-localized, producing citramalate above WT background. 3. Condensate targeting increased citramalate yield by ~2.3-fold compared with diffuse cytosolic MdCMS. 4. Condensate targeting also increased MdCMS accumulation, suggesting improved enzyme stability contributes to higher product output.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No explicit growth penalty reported; WT leaves showed a small background citramalate signal; direct citramalate sequestration inside condensates was not demonstrated
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful heterologous enzyme for boosting acetyl-CoA-derived product synthesis in N. benthamiana; condensate targeting may improve stability and yield of single-enzyme pathways
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The native chloroplast transit peptide was removed to retain enzyme in the cytosol for condensate recruitment; authors hypothesize enhanced stability is likely the main driver of increased yield

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in agroinfiltrated mature leaves; product assessed after reinfiltration and 13 days total expression
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: β-ketothiolase; thiolase family; fused to SYNZIP2 and detectable via Myc tag in some constructs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of acetyl-CoA to form acetoacetyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Biodegradable carbon-storage polymer with industrial bioplastic value
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Engineered cytosolic biomolecular condensates in leaf cells; PHB granules co-localized with condensate scaffold by Nile red staining
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium co-infiltration with scaffold and client constructs; P19 co-infiltration; re-infiltration at day 5; PHB measured at 13 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: As part of the three-enzyme PHB pathway, remained functional when condensate-targeted and contributed to ~4.9-fold higher PHB accumulation than diffuse cytosolic pathway localization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; mCherry-based co-localization tests; PHB quantification by GC-MS; Nile red staining; immunoblotting; comparison of +RGG versus -RGG scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhbA could be recruited to cytosolic synthetic condensates together with the other PHB enzymes. 2. The PHB pathway remained functional when PhbA was condensate-targeted. 3. Condensate targeting of the PHB pathway increased PHB accumulation by ~4.9-fold over non-condensate controls. 4. PhbA accumulation was strongly increased in the +RGG condition, consistent with enzyme stabilization.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No explicit growth penalty reported; PHB production is noted as potentially detrimental in hosts because of hydrophobic PHB granules, which condensate sequestration may alleviate
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering higher PHB bioplastic production in plant biofactories, especially when combined with condensate-based enzyme stabilization and product sequestration
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tested within a polycistronic client system separated by InteinF2A; recruitment verified using mCherry-tagged enzyme variants

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in agroinfiltrated mature leaves; product assessed after reinfiltration and 13 days total expression
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Acetoacetyl-CoA reductase; short-chain dehydrogenase/reductase family; fused to SYNZIP2
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Reduction step in PHB monomer synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Biodegradable carbon-storage polymer with industrial bioplastic value
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Engineered cytosolic biomolecular condensates in leaf cells; PHB granules co-localized with condensate scaffold by Nile red staining
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium co-infiltration with scaffold and client constructs; P19 co-infiltration; re-infiltration at day 5; PHB measured at 13 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: As part of the three-enzyme PHB pathway, remained functional when condensate-targeted and contributed to ~4.9-fold higher PHB accumulation than diffuse cytosolic pathway localization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; mCherry-based co-localization tests; PHB quantification by GC-MS; Nile red staining; immunoblotting with anti-PhbB; comparison of +RGG versus -RGG scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhbB could be recruited to cytosolic synthetic condensates with the other PHB enzymes present. 2. The condensate-localized PHB pathway remained catalytically functional. 3. Condensate targeting increased final PHB accumulation by ~4.9-fold. 4. PhbB protein was detectable in +RGG but not -RGG lysates, indicating markedly greater accumulation/stability when condensate-targeted.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No explicit growth penalty reported; pathway depends on NADPH and acetyl-CoA availability
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports engineering of more stable and productive PHB pathways in transient plant systems using condensate localization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Detected directly by anti-PhbB immunoblot in crude lysates

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in agroinfiltrated mature leaves; product assessed after reinfiltration and 13 days total expression
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PHB synthase; PHA synthase family; fused to SYNZIP2 and HA tag in some constructs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Biodegradable carbon-storage polymer with industrial bioplastic value
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Engineered cytosolic biomolecular condensates in leaf cells; PHB granules co-localized with condensate scaffold by Nile red staining
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium co-infiltration with scaffold and client constructs; P19 co-infiltration; re-infiltration at day 5; PHB measured at 13 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: As part of the three-enzyme PHB pathway, remained functional when condensate-targeted and contributed to ~4.9-fold higher PHB accumulation than diffuse cytosolic pathway localization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; mCherry-based co-localization tests; PHB quantification by GC-MS; Nile red staining; semi-quantitative fluorescence; anti-HA immunoblotting; comparison of +RGG versus -RGG scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhbC could be recruited to cytosolic synthetic condensates with the other PHB enzymes present. 2. PHB accumulated only after extended expression/re-infiltration, indicating the condensate-localized pathway is functional but slow. 3. Condensate targeting increased PHB yield by ~4.9-fold over non-condensate controls. 4. PhbC showed up to 7.4-fold higher accumulation in the +RGG condition, strongly supporting condensate-mediated protein stabilization.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No explicit growth penalty reported; PHB is hydrophobic and may stress hosts, though sequestration in condensates may reduce this burden
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Especially valuable terminal enzyme for improving PHB bioplastic accumulation in N. benthamiana through condensate targeting
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper highlights PhbC as an example in protein accumulation imaging; EC number not explicitly stated in text
